# Pavin Das – Security Engineer & Ethical Hacker

> Primary focus: Web Application Security Testing, Vulnerability Assessment, and Secure Code Review
> Pavin is a cybersecurity professional based in Bengaluru, specializing in identifying application vulnerabilities, auditing source code, and designing automated defensive monitoring systems.

Last updated: 2026-05-29

---

## What We Help With
- Vulnerable Web Applications → Hardened systems through comprehensive manual and automated security testing.
- Insecure Source Code → Secure codebases with early detection of insecure design patterns before deployment.
- Infrastructure Exposures → Reduced attack surface through vulnerability assessments and defensive configuration audits.

---

## Who We Serve
- Tech Startups & SaaS Providers seeking to secure their applications prior to production release.
- Development Teams needing external code review, vulnerability mitigation guides, and remediation advice.
- Security Operations Centers looking to automate log monitoring, alert parsing, and incident response.

---

## Why Choose Us
- Practical Tooling Expertise: Developed and published security tools like WiFried (wireless attacks), TrafficTrace (traffic analysis), and TamperTracker (integrity monitoring).
- Dual offensive & defensive skill set: Proficient in vulnerability exploitation (BurpSuite, Caido, Nmap) as well as defensive orchestration (Wazuh SIEM, Shuffle SOAR, OPNsense firewalls).
- Proven execution: Successfully completed 30+ security assessment, tool design, and lab automation projects.

---

## Services
- [Web Application Testing](https://pavindas.github.io/#services): Manual and automated security testing for modern web apps.
- [Secure Code Review](https://pavindas.github.io/#services): Identify insecure patterns before they reach production.
- [Vulnerability Assessment](https://pavindas.github.io/#services): End-to-end assessments with prioritized remediation steps.

---

## Proof & Projects
- [WiFried GitHub Repository](https://github.com/PavinDas/WiFried): WiFi attacking and handshake capturing tool.
- [TrafficTrace GitHub Repository](https://github.com/PavinDas/TrafficTrace): Network traffic monitor and intrusion detection utility.
- [TamperTracker GitHub Repository](https://github.com/PavinDas/TamperTracker): File Integrity Monitor with automatic Jira ticketing.
- [NopeWare GitHub Repository](https://github.com/PavinDas/NopeWare): CLI virus scanner leveraging VirusTotal APIs.
- [SubmarineScanner GitHub Repository](https://github.com/PavinDas/Subdomains_Finder.git): Lightweight subdomain finder with custom wordlists.
- [Technical Walkthroughs](https://pavindas.github.io/blog.html): Hands-on security tutorials, home lab configurations, and CTF writeups.

---

## Technical Blogs
- [The CIA Triad](https://pavindas.github.io/blogs/cia-triad.html): A narrative guide to the core pillars of cybersecurity: Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability.
- [ELK Stack Setup & Configuration Guide](https://pavindas.github.io/blogs/elk-stack.html): Step-by-step setup of Elasticsearch, Logstash, and Kibana on Ubuntu for log management.
- [Building a Home SOC Lab with Wazuh and Shuffle](https://pavindas.github.io/blogs/soc-lab.html): Detailed walkthrough of setting up a SOC lab using VirtualBox, OPNsense, Wazuh, and Shuffle.
- [Penetration Test Report: Mr. Robot](https://pavindas.github.io/blogs/mr-robot.html): Full CTF walkthrough covering enumeration, WordPress theme editing exploitation (RCE), and privilege escalation via SUID Nmap.

---
